_meta.description: Integrated exam for Module 09 CEP — Pattern API, MATCH_RECOGNIZE, NFA, time semantics, real-world

questions.question: Pattern API: Pattern.<E>begin('a').where(p1).followedBy('b').where(p2). На sequence [A1, X, A2, X, B1, B2] какие matches эмитятся (default contiguity, AFTER MATCH SKIP PAST LAST EVENT)?

options.text: (A1, B1), (A1, B2), (A2, B1), (A2, B2) — все combinations
(A1, B1) — followedBy single match: A1 завершается на first B (=B1), потом SKIP PAST LAST EVENT, дальше нет A для второго match

questions.explanation: followedBy — relaxed contiguity, single match per A. На [A1, X, A2, X, B1, B2]: A1 matched as 'a', X ignored, A2 starts second partial match (also 'a'), X ignored, B1 matches 'b' — first partial match (A1, B1) completed. AFTER MATCH SKIP PAST LAST EVENT puts cursor после B1, остальные partial matches discarded. Дальше B2, нет A — no match. followedByAny эмитил бы все 4 combinations. consecutive не применим к followedBy.

questions.question: MATCH_RECOGNIZE: PATTERN (A B+ C), DEFINE B AS B.price < PREV(price). Что означает PREV(price) здесь?

options.text: Цена предыдущего events который matched B
Цена physical previous row in ORDER BY — независимо от того matched ли previous row. Это даёт 'текущая цена ниже physical previous' — natural для downtrend detection

questions.explanation: Без variable PREV(price) references physical previous row in ORDER BY — это всегда predecessor independent of matching. Альтернатива PREV(B.price) — previous row которая matched variable B (skip-ит над non-B rows). Для downtrend detection PREV(price) natural — 'current cheaper than immediately preceding'. PREV(B.price) даёт 'current cheaper than last B' что redundant (current = B уже). Common pitfall — confusing эти две semantics, ведёт к subtle bugs.

questions.question: Production: CEP job показывает growing checkpoint duration (10s → 60s → 300s). Pattern: Pattern.<E>begin('a').where(...).oneOrMore().followedByAny('b').where(...) без within. Что fix?

options.text: Главная причина — unbounded partial matches state из-за: (1) отсутствия within() → partial matches накапливаются forever; (2) followedByAny — cartesian product N×M matches; (3) oneOrMore greedy. Fix: добавить within(Duration), заменить followedByAny на followedBy (если не нужны overlapping), bound oneOrMore через times(min, max)

questions.explanation: Это classic CEP production deterioration. Чек все три факторы: unbounded within → state grows linearly per key forever; followedByAny → N×M combinatorial matches при busy keys; oneOrMore greedy → каждый matching event extends partial match. Combined эффект — exponential state growth, checkpoint duration scales с state size, eventually OOM. Fix: tight within(), prefer followedBy unless explicit need overlapping, bound через times(min,max). Если эти изменения compromise semantics — migrate к custom state machine.

questions.question: Use case: 'IoT cascade alarm: device emits HIGH_TEMP, потом within 5 min same device emits SHUTDOWN, потом within 10 min adjacent device emits HIGH_TEMP'. Какие approaches?

options.text: Pure Pattern API — все в одном pattern

Hybrid: упрощённый Pattern API/MATCH_RECOGNIZE для per-device sequence (HIGH_TEMP → SHUTDOWN within 5 min) + downstream KeyedProcessFunction keyBy(area) для cross-device correlation. Per-device CEP не может correlate adjacent devices structurally

questions.explanation: Three-step pattern имеет mix per-device (steps 1-2) + cross-device (step 3 — adjacent device). Pattern API runs per-key (deviceId) — может handle steps 1-2, но cross-device correlation для adjacent device требует joining streams from different keys. Production architecture: layer 1 — per-device CEP detects HIGH_TEMP + SHUTDOWN sequence, emits 'device-down' alerts. Layer 2 — KeyedProcessFunction keyBy(area) собирает 'device-down' alerts + tracks recent HIGH_TEMP events from adjacent devices через ValueState, applies cross-device condition.

questions.question: В каком случае выбрать AFTER MATCH SKIP TO NEXT ROW вместо SKIP PAST LAST ROW?

options.text: Когда хотите все overlapping matches: каждый row может быть start нового match. Например 'every 3-event subset showing downtrend' — нужны overlapping triplets. Caveat: pattern не должен admit empty match (PATTERN (A?) с TO NEXT ROW → runtime error из infinite loop)

questions.explanation: SKIP TO NEXT ROW — для use cases когда хотите все overlapping sub-matches. Например finance analytics 'every triplet showing downtrend' — sliding window over всех matching triplets. SKIP PAST LAST ROW даёт disjoint matches (skips весь matched range), теряются overlapping. Trade-off: TO NEXT ROW даёт significantly больше matches → больше state, больше output. Constraint: pattern должен require non-empty match — иначе infinite loop, Flink throws compile error. Production usage rare — обычно SKIP PAST LAST ROW correct.

questions.question: Best production CEP architecture обычно combines layers: upstream enrichment (KeyedProcessFunction со state) → Pattern API/MATCH_RECOGNIZE для standard temporal sequences → downstream correlator (KeyedProcessFunction для cross-key/multi-source correlation).
questions.explanation: Правда. Production-grade CEP architectures rarely pure Pattern API. Layered approach: (1) enrichment layer добавляет context (is_new_ip, fraud_score, user_segment) через KeyedProcessFunction со ValueState; (2) CEP layer применяет declarative patterns на enriched events; (3) correlator layer joins cross-key state, applies dynamic rules через broadcast state. Каждый layer focuses на одной concern — easier to maintain, scale, debug. Pure Pattern API ограничен structurally (no async I/O, no cross-key, no dynamic patterns), pure state machine — verbose for simple sequences. Combine для best of both.
